item.title: 关于 JitPack 使用 git 仓库名作为 Maven artifactId | On JitPack's use of git repository name as Maven artifactId
item.description:
文森特·A·西西雷洛

发表于除此之外，在本系列的前两篇文章中，我们看到 JitPack 是一种不同类型的 Maven 存储库，因为它在任何人第一次将特定版本导入他们的项目时按需在 JitPack 服务器上构建您的工件，并且它从 GitHub（或 Bitbucket 或其他 git 主机）存储库中的源代码执行此操作。 虽然您可以将常规反向域配置为groupId ，如我之前的帖子中所见，该帖子还解释了如何使 JitPack 提前构建，但默认情况下，JitPack 根据 git 主机和该 git 主机上的用户定义groupId （例如， com.github.USER ）。 目录：

为什么这是一个限制

你刚才不是说吗？ 是的。 我重复了它，因为我认为这是一个潜在的限制，主要是针对像我这样已经在其他地方发布了带有工件的库的人（例如，我在 Maven Central 上发布了我的所有库），但我也已经开始使用 JitPack 了能够从特定的提交、分支、拉取请求等构建快照。有关此内容的更多信息，请参见该系列的第一篇文章，包括为最近的 JDK 版本配置 JitPack 。

那么为什么这是一个限制呢？ 好吧，对于我的一些库，我已经用于其他存储库（例如 Maven Central）的artifactId与 GitHub 存储库名称相同。 在其他情况下，唯一的区别在于套管。 这没什么大不了的，因为在引用 GitHub 存储库名称时，大小写并不重要。 它只是浏览 GitHub 时的视觉元素。

但是，在其他几种情况下会出现问题，其中我有一个现有库，其中包含许多发布的artifactId版本，其中工件 ID 与 GitHub 存储库名称不同。 重命名 GitHub 存储库会破坏指向它的现有链接。 在 Maven Central 上切换到不同的artifactId以匹配存储库名称会导致混淆，因为旧版本会有一个artifactId而新版本会有不同的 artifactId。

由于这个限制，我有一些尚未为 JitPack 构建配置的库。 其中之一是JavaPermutationTools ，其 GitHub 存储库名称为JavaPermutationTools但 Maven artifactId为jpt 。 实际上，这个特定的库也使用了 Java 平台模块系统 (JPMS)，并提供了一个名为org.cicirello.jpt的 JPMS 模块，它恰好匹配我的反向域groupId和artifactId的组合，虽然没有实际要求这样做。 我确实将反向域配置为在 JitPack 上使用，因此groupId将在我提供库的所有 Maven 存储库中保持一致。 但是，拥有一个不同的artifactId让我很困扰。 如果我要配置 JitPack 构建（在这种情况下是必要的，因为这个库需要 JDK 17 并且默认情况下使用 JDK 8 构建 JitPack），那么如果从 Maven Central 与 JitPack 进行导入，导入看起来会有所不同。

假设的 JitPack 导入：但是从 JitPack 中，它必然是（因为我不会重命名存储库）：

 <dependency> <groupId> org.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> JavaPermutationTools </artifactId> <version> 4.1.0 </version> </dependency>

这里混淆的可能性太高了，因为依赖定义看起来是不同的工件，而实际上它们包含相同的 Java 模块和相同的 Java 包集。

小费

如果您刚刚开始使用 Java（或 Kotlin 或其他 JVM 语言）库，并且您可能希望将工件发布到多个 Maven 存储库（包括 JitPack），那么将您的 git 存储库命名为与您的artifactId相同成为。 事实上，即使您不打算特意支持 JitPack，因为无论库的维护者（例如，您）是否打算这样做，都可以从 JitPack 导入按需构建的工件（前提是 JitPack 的默认构建适用于您library)，那么无论如何将存储库名称与您选择的artifactId匹配可能是一个好主意。

示例中使用的项目

这些示例基于JavaPermutationTools ，这是一个 Java 库，专注于与排列和序列相关的所有类型的计算。 除其他外，它包括大量用于计算两个排列之间距离的距离度量，以及用于计算字符串对或其他顺序结构（如数组）之间距离的字符串距离度量。 我可能会写关于图书馆的未来DEV帖子。 如果有机会，请查看 JPT。

这是它的 GitHub 存储库：

版权所有 (C) 2018-2022文森特 A. Cicirello 。

网站： https ://jpt.cicirello.org/

关于图书馆的出版物 软件包和版本 构建状态 安全 执照 支持

如何引用

如果您在研究中使用此库，请引用以下论文：

开源软件杂志，3(31)，950。https ://doi.org/10.21105/joss.00950 。

概述

它包括各种排列距离度量以及序列上的距离度量（即字符串数组和其他有序数据类型）的实现。

我们目前支持 Java 17+。 有关库版本和支持的最低 Java 之间的映射，请参见下表...

在哪里可以找到我

在网上：

卡内基梅隆大学机器人学博士。 他是 ACM 高级会员、IEEE 高级会员、AAAI 终身会员、EAI 杰出会员和 SIAM 会员。

在 DEV 上关注我：

在 GitHub 上关注我：

可以找到我或我的作品的网站
网络和社交媒体
软件开发
出版物

如果您想为您自己的 GitHub 配置文件生成与上述等效的内容，请查看cicirello/user-statistician GitHub 操作。




一旦暂停，cicirello 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，cicirello 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，cicirello 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 cicirello 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 cicirello 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 cicirello 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Vincent A. Cicirello

Posted on Aug 18

This next installment of the series "JitPack Tips and Tricks" again deals with JitPack, but will be much shorter than the prior two. Among other things, in the prior two posts of this series, we saw that JitPack is a different type of Maven repository in that it builds your artifacts on JitPack servers on-demand the first time anyone imports a particular version into their project, and it does so from the source code from the GitHub (or Bitbucket or other git host) repository. Although you can configure the conventional reverse domain as the groupId, as seen in my previous post, which also explained how to make JitPack build ahead-of-time, by default JitPack defines the groupId based on the git host and user on that git host (e.g., com.github.USER). JitPack then uses the name of the git repository as the artifactId, regardless of what is specified in your pom.xml (it renames whatever jar files the build produces).

Table of Contents:

Why This is a Limitation

JitPack uses the name of the git repository as the artifactId. Didn't you just say that? Yes. I repeated it because I see this as a potential limitation, mainly to someone like me who already has libraries with artifacts published elsewhere (e.g., I publish all of my libraries on Maven Central), but I've begun using JitPack as well for its ability to build snapshots from specific commits, branches, pull-requests, etc. See first post in the series for more on this, including configuring JitPack for recent JDK versions.

So why is this a limitation? Well, for some of my libraries, the artifactId I'm already using for other repositories such as Maven Central is identical to the GitHub repository name. In other cases, the only difference is in casing. The artifactId is conventionally written in all lowercase (a convention I follow), but the corresponding GitHub repository name is written in mixed-case for some of my libraries. This is no big deal because casing doesn't matter when referencing a GitHub repository name. It is just a visual element when browsing GitHub.

However, an issue arises in a couple other cases, where I have an existing library with many releases of artifacts published with an artifactId to Maven Central that is different than the GitHub repository name. Renaming the GitHub repository would break existing links to it. Switching to a different artifactId on Maven Central to match the repository name would cause confusion, since older versions would have one artifactId and newer versions a different one.

Due to this limitation, I have a few libraries that I have not yet configured for JitPack builds. One of these is JavaPermutationTools, with a GitHub repository name of JavaPermutationTools but Maven artifactId of jpt. Actually, this specific library also uses the Java Platform Module System (JPMS), and provides a JPMS module named org.cicirello.jpt, which happens to match the combination of my reverse domain groupId and the artifactId, although there is no actual requirement to do so. I do have my reverse domain configured for use on JitPack, so the groupId will be consistent across all Maven repositories where I make my library available. But, having an artifactId that differs bothers me. If I were to configure JitPack builds (necessary in this case because this library requires JDK 17 and JitPack by default builds with JDK 8), then importing would look different if done from Maven Central vs JitPack.

Maven Dependency: Importing from Maven Central currently involves specifying the dependency with:

<dependency> <groupId>org.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>jpt</artifactId> <version>4.1.0</version>
</dependency>

Hypothetical JitPack Import: But from JitPack, it would necessarily be (since I won't rename repository):

<dependency> <groupId>org.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>JavaPermutationTools</artifactId> <version>4.1.0</version>
</dependency>

The potential for confusion is too high here since the dependency definitions appear to be different artifacts, when in fact they contain the same Java module, and the same set of Java packages.

Tip

If you are just beginning a Java (or Kotlin or other JVM language) library, and if there is a chance that you might want to release artifacts to multiple Maven repositories, including JitPack, then name your git repository the same as you intend your artifactId to be. In fact, even if you don't plan on deliberately supporting JitPack, since it is possible to import artifacts built on-demand from JitPack whether the library's maintainers (e.g., you) intend for this or not (provided JitPack's default build works for your library), then it might be a good idea anyway to match your repository name to your chosen artifactId.

Project Used in Examples

The examples are based on JavaPermutationTools, which is a Java library that is focused on all types of computation related to permutations as well as sequences. It includes, among other things, an extensive collection of distance metrics for calculating the distance between two permutations, as well as string distance metrics for calculating the distance between pairs of strings or other sequential structures, such as arrays. I may write future DEV posts about the library. Check out JPT if you have the chance.

JPT has a website:

The JavaPermutationTools (JPT) library includes a variety of permutation distance metrics as well as distance metrics on sequences (i.e., Strings, arrays, and other ordered data types).

And here is it's GitHub repository:

Copyright (C) 2018-2022 Vincent A. Cicirello.

Website: https://jpt.cicirello.org/

API documentation: https://jpt.cicirello.org/api

Publications About the Library
Packages and Releases
Build Status
JaCoCo Test Coverage
Security
DOI
License
Support

How to Cite

If you use this library in your research, please cite the following paper:

Cicirello, Vincent A (2018). JavaPermutationTools: A Java Library of Permutation Distance Metrics. Journal of Open Source Software, 3(31), 950. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.00950 .

Overview

The JavaPermutationTools (JPT) library provides Java classes and interfaces, etc that enable representing and generating permutations and sequences, as well as performing computation on permutations and sequences. It includes implementations of a variety of permutation distance metrics as well as distance metrics on sequences (i.e., Strings arrays, and other ordered data types).

Java 17+

We currently support Java 17+. See the following table for mapping between library version and minimum supported Java…

Where You Can Find Me

On the Web:

Vincent A. Cicirello - Professor of Computer Science at Stockton University - is a researcher in artificial intelligence, evolutionary computation, swarm intelligence, and computational intelligence, with a Ph.D. in Robotics from Carnegie Mellon University. He is an ACM Senior Member, IEEE Senior Member, AAAI Life Member, EAI Distinguished Member, and SIAM Member.

Follow me here on DEV:

Follow me on GitHub:

Sites where you can find me or my work
Web and social media
Software development
Publications

If you want to generate the equivalent to the above for your own GitHub profile, check out the cicirello/user-statistician GitHub Action.

Red Ochsenbein (he/him) - Aug 11

item.title: 学习 JavaScript 的绝对初学者指南，第 1 部分 | Absolute Beginner's Guide to Learn JavaScript, Part-1
item.description:
正如我的朋友所说，“如果你了解 JavaScript，你可以做任何事情，甚至可以用它来构建游戏。” 在我作为软件工程师的生活中，我遇到最多的问题是：

  • 如何学习 JavaScript？
  • 我知道如何在 JavaScript 中声明变量或如何编写循环，但是当我不得不处理浏览器代码时，我感到不知所措。
  • 我想学习 React 或 Next JS，但我不懂 JavaScript。 该怎么办？

当我开始从事前端 Web 开发时，我只是被告知要学习 JavaScript，因为它是必不可少的。 作为一名刚毕业的实习生，我所做的就是花时间了解“const”和“let”关键字之间的区别或学习每个循环，然后我被分配学习 React，几周后我就一无所获。 它很快就变成了在 JavaScript 和 React 之间来回切换（Giddyup，Kramer 的灾难秘诀）。

经过这么多年，我正在考虑将我的经验和观察结合起来，以一种让你在学习前端 Web 开发的同时获得优势的方式来学习 JavaScript。 我不是专业人士，但我知道交易的技巧，以及人们在开始使用此筹码时所面临的绝对恐吓。 如果您刚刚学会了如何打印“Hello World”或弄乱 Wiki 条目，那么这个博客已经为您提供了帮助。

首先，如果您打算学习前端 Web 开发的基础知识，则需要学习 HTML CSS 和 JavaScript，并开始访问Free Code Camp 。 这个网站是一个宝石，您可以同时学习和应用您的知识，而且它是免费的！ 一旦你到达这个或任何教程网站的 JavaScript 和/或 React（或任何前端框架）部分，事情往往会变得复杂。 在我保证的本系列课程中，我将尝试弥补以下部分中的空白。 今天，我将为您提供一些在构建网站或 Web 应用程序时需要用到的关键 JavaScript 概念的备忘单。 开始：

任何网页都是包含元素的 HTML 标记的组合。 转到您的浏览器并单击左键。 您将在底部看到“inspect”或“inspect element”，单击它，您可以看到一连串的 HTML 和 CSS。

这是我的 chrome 起始页的DOM或文档对象模型。 每个网页都是一个 HTML DOM 模型，它被构造为一棵对象树。 树如下所示，图片由 [W3 Schools] 提供：( https://www.w3schools.com/whatis/whatis_htmldom.asp )

我们使用 JavaScript 所做的是，我们尝试为这些 HTML 元素提供指令，或者从中获得一些东西。 这就是 JavaScript 使网站动态化的方式。 这些指令由标签名、类名、id、CSS 选择器或单个 HTML 元素的 HTML 对象集合给出。

假设有一个如下标签：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <body> <h1 id="heading1">Kramerica Industries</h1> </body> </html>

为了改变标题的颜色，我们可以做的是：

 ... </body> <script> const h = document.getElementById("heading1"); h.style.color = "red"; </script> </html>

输出将如下所示：

如果h1标记有一个类而不是一个 id，则您必须编写getElementByClassName而不是getElementById 。 有关这些功能的列表，请参阅W3 Schools的此链接。

假设您要更改段落标签的文本。 你可以做的是：

 ... <p>Paragraph 1</p> <p>Paragraph 2</p> </body> <script> ... document.getElementsByTagName("p")[0].innerHTML = "Art Vandaley"; </script> </html>

在这里，我们使用innerHTML属性用另一个文本覆盖了第一段。

由于我对 DOM 操作一无所知，所以我对 JavaScript 的了解相当模糊，所以我在 React 上苦苦挣扎。 为了完全掌握 JavaScript 的这些概念，Wes Bos 有一个 30 天的最新课程 - 30 天 JavaScript 。 此外，W3Schools 是你学习 JavaScript 和网站开发的朋友，所以当你遇到困难时，一定要谷歌。

如果要在浏览器中打印某些内容，则需要调用浏览器控制台的 log 方法，例如：

在我们的代码中，在脚本标签内写一条消息，然后检查你的控制台：

 ... <script> ... console.log("Bania is a hack!") </script> </html>

输出将如下所示：

作为前端开发人员，您将始终必须编写显示在浏览器中的代码。 因此，如果您经常控制变量和对象的输出和类型，这是一个最佳实践。 这是一个例子：

 ... <script> const h = document.getElementById("heading1"); h.style.color = "red"; console.log("check type:", typeof(h)) </script> </html>

变量 h 的类型显示在控制台中：

如您所见，包含h1标签元素的变量 h 的类型是object 。 这是因为这个元素现在是DOM树中的一个对象。

在 JavaScript 中有三种声明变量的方法——使用关键字“let”、“const”和“var”。 与其他编程语言不同，不需要显式地写出变量的类型，比如在C语言中你需要写“int c”来声明一个整数类型的变量c 。 除了Object，都是原始数据类型。 所有原始数据类型也都有 Object 对应物，您可以通过使用“new”关键字创建构造函数来使用它们； 这种风格很少被遵循。

现在，使用 const 声明一个变量，以后使用时不会重新分配。 与 const 不同，您可以重新分配 let 变量。 问题是，什么是块作用域？

看看这段代码：

 ... <script> { let a = 10 const b = 100 } console.log(a) console.log(b) </script> </html>

对于任何一个变量，控制台都会抛出如下错误：

为什么？ 因为变量 a 和 b 在花括号之外不存在。 这称为变量的块作用域。

使用“var”关键字声明的变量具有函数作用域，这意味着该变量在整个函数中都可用。 考虑以下示例：

 ... <script> function hello(){ var x = 5 function world(){ var y = 8 console.log("inside",x) console.log("inside",y) } world() console.log("outside",x) console.log("outside",y) } hello() </script>

输出：

为什么 y 在其函数世界之外存在错误？ 因为 y 的范围受其功能的约束。 结果，我们可以得到 x 而不是 y。

现在我们了解了 JavaScript 中变量作用域的工作原理，我们需要了解一个有趣的地方。 如何？ 解释器将所有声明移动到其作用域的顶部。 这是一个问题。 仅当您使用var关键字声明变量时，提升才有效。

 <script> a = 6 console.log( 6 * 6 ) var a </script>

如果你用let尝试它，你会得到一个错误，因为在初始化之前访问一个变量。 并且 const 不允许您以这种方式声明变量。

我希望我能够为学习 JavaScript 以进行前端 Web 开发奠定一些基础。 在接下来的文章中，我将写一些更多的 JavaScript 概念，这些概念对于任何 JS 框架（如 React）来说都是基础的。 再见！



原文:

JavaScript is a versatile language. As my friend says, "If you know JavaScript, you can do anything, even you can build a game with this." In my life as a software engineer, the questions that I come across the most are:

  • How to learn Javascript?
  • I know how to declare a variable in JavaScript or how to write a loop, but I feel overwhelmed when I have to deal with browser code.
  • I want to learn React or Next JS but I don't know JavaScript. What to do?

While I was starting in front-end web development, I was just told to learn JavaScript because it's indispensable. Being a fresh graduate intern, what I did was spent time knowing the difference between "const" and "let" keywords or learning for each loop, and then I was assigned to learn React which after some weeks fell flat on me. It soon became a back and forth between JavaScript and React (Giddyup, Kramer's recipe for disasters right there).

After all these years, I'm thinking of putting together my experience and observations to learn JavaScript in a way that gives you an edge while learning front-end web development. I'm not a pro, but I know the tricks of the trade, as well as the sheer intimidation that people face while they are starting in this stack. If you just learned how to print "Hello World" or mess with Wiki entries, this blog has got you covered.

First, if you are planning to learn the basics of front-end web development, you need to learn HTML CSS and JavaScript, and to kickstart visit Free Code Camp. This website is a gem where you can learn and apply your knowledge simultaneously, also it’s free! Things tend to get convoluted once you reach the JavaScript and/or React (or any frontend framework) section of this or any tutorial site. I will be trying to bridge the gaps in the following sections over the course of this series I promise. Today, I will provide you with a cheat-sheet of some of the crucial JavaScript concepts that you will be needing while building a website or a web app. Here goes:

1. HTML Element manipulation

Any web page is a combination of HTML tags that are holding the elements. Go to your browser and click left. You will see “inspect” or “inspect element” at the bottom, click it and you can see a litany of HTML and CSS.

This is the DOM or Document Object Model of my chrome start page. Every web page is a HTML DOM model which is constructed as a tree of Objects. The tree looks like the following, image courtesy [W3 Schools] :(https://www.w3schools.com/whatis/whatis_htmldom.asp)

The HTML DOM is an API (Programming Interface) for JavaScript. What we do with JavaScript is, we try to give instructions to these HTML elements, or get something out of this. That’s how JavaScript makes a website dynamic. The instructions are given by tag name, class name, id, CSS selectors or HTML object collections of individual HTML elements.

Let’s say there is a tag like the following:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>

<h1 id="heading1">Kramerica Industries</h1>

</body>
</html>

In order to change the color of the heading, what we can do is:

...

</body>
<script>
const h = document.getElementById("heading1");
h.style.color = "red";
</script>
</html>

The output will look like the following :

If the h1 tag had a class instead of an id, you would have to write getElementByClassName instead of getElementById . Refer to this link of W3 Schools for a list of these functions.

Let's say that you want to change the text of a paragraph tag. What you can do is:

...

<p>Paragraph 1</p>
<p>Paragraph 2</p>

</body>
<script>

...

document.getElementsByTagName("p")[0].innerHTML = "Art Vandaley";

</script>
</html>

innerHTML property sets or returns the HTML content of an element. Here, we had overwritten the first paragraph with another text with the use of innerHTML property.

As my knowledge of JavaScript was rather ambiguous because I had no idea of the DOM manipulation, I was struggling with React. To fully grasp these conecepts of JavaScript, Wes Bos has a 30 day course which is up-to-date - 30 Day JavaScript. Also, W3Schools is your friend to learn JavaScript and website development, so definitely google when you hit a snag.

2. Writing on the Browser Console

If you want to print something in the browser, you need to call the log method of browser console like:

console.log(“Write something“)

In our code, write a message inside the script tag and then check your console:

...

<script>
...

console.log("Bania is a hack!") 

</script>
</html>

The output will look like this:

As a front-end developer, you will always have to write code which is shown in the browser. Therefore, it's a best practice if you frequently console the outputs and types of your variables and objects. Here's an example:


...
<script>
const h = document.getElementById("heading1");
h.style.color = "red";

console.log("check type:", typeof(h))

</script>
</html>

The type of variable h is shown in the console:

As you can see, the type of variable h which contains the element of h1 tag is object. It's because this element is now an object in the DOM tree.

3. Variable Scopes and Hoisting

There are three ways to declare a variable in JavaScript - using the keywords “let”, “const” and “var”. Unlike other programming languages, there is no need to explicitly write the type of the variable, such as in C you need to write “int c” to declare a variable c of integer type. JavaScript has data types such as String, Int, Boolean, Null, Undefined, and Object. Except for Object, all are primitive data types. All the primitive data types have also Object counterparts which you can use by creating a constructor with "new" keyword; this style is rarely followed.

Now, with const you declare a variable which you won’t re-assign in future use. A const variable is block scoped, and so is a variable declared with the “let” keyword. Unlike const, you can reassign a let variable. Question is, what is a block-scope?

Look at this code:

...
<script>


{
let a = 10
const b = 100

}

console.log(a) 
console.log(b) 
</script>
</html>

For either of the variable, the console will throw error like the following:

Why? Because variable a and b do not exist outside the curly braces. This is called a block scope for a variable.

A variable that is declared using "var" keyword has a function scope, meaning the variable is available across the whole function. Consider the following example:

...
<script>
function hello(){
    var x = 5

    function world(){
       var y = 8
       console.log("inside",x)
       console.log("inside",y)
}

world()

console.log("outside",x)
console.log("outside",y)
}

hello()

</script>

Output:

Why is there an error for y outside its function world? Because the scope of y is bound by its function here. As a result, we can get x outside but not y.

Now that we understand how variable scopes work in JavaScript, we need to understand an interesting bit. JavaScript allows variable hoisting, meaning you can use a variable or function or class in JavsScript even before declaring it. How? The interpreter moves all the declaration to the top of of their scopes. Here's a catch. Hoisting only works if you declare the variable with var keyword.

<script>

a = 6

console.log( 6 * 6 )

var a

</script>

If you try it with let, you will get an error as for accessing a variable before initialization. And const does not allow you to declare variable this way.

I hope I have been able to lay down some ground works for learning JavaScript for front-end web development. In the following post I will write about some more JavaScript concepts that are also fundamental for any JS framework such as React. Byeeeeeee!
